title: Ensure --find searches fresh index; reindex before each search
context: Runbooks written out-of-band (MCP on another machine, direct JSON edits) were invisible to --find until another command triggered an index rebuild. Users expected search to show current state.
decision: Add unconditional reindex() call before searchRunbooks() in --find mode, mirroring the behavior of hunch query. Accept the reindex cost for consistency.
consequences: --find now always reflects out-of-band changes (runbooks added/edited externally)
Behavior consistent with hunch query; single mental model for 'search includes recent edits'
Small performance overhead on every --find invocation (reindex is cheap relative to search latency)
alternatives_rejected: Document that --find requires manual reindex; shift burden to user
Add --force flag to trigger reindex; adds option proliferation and complexity
Track index freshness (mtime, revision counter) and reindex only if stale; more state to maintain

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Document that --find requires manual reindex; shift burden to user
Add --force flag to trigger reindex; adds option proliferation and complexity
Track index freshness (mtime, revision counter) and reindex only if stale; more state to maintain
